mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in unemployment below 7% signals labor market normalization, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.0% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being crossed.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized expansion in construction and broader employment, reinforcing corporate earnings momentum and lifting S&P 500 over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 40% of observations across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production accelerates signals capacity utilization improvements that immediately translate into higher equity valuations during periods of tight supply constraints.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO prints coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation above 3% forces market pricing of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Inflation prints above threshold consistently yield positive 20-day equity returns across multiple inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns in a rolling 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm employment confirm labor market tightening, reducing wage inflation fears while supporting consumption, thereby lifting equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint labor improvement fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, prompting immediate downward revisions to corporate profit forecasts and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Retail sales contractions are followed by positive 5-day equity returns in more than 55% of cases across multiple recessions.
